title: Brayan Bello excels in bulk relief, but opener melts in Red Sox’ 4-3 loss to Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Tyler Samaniego allowed four runs in the first inning, contributing to the Red Sox's 4-3 loss to the Guardians.
Brayan Bello excelled in relief, pitching seven scoreless innings with five strikeouts and no walks.
The Red Sox, now 23-33, have lost six of their last seven games and remain last in the AL East.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo praises TD Garden environment amid trade rumors
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised Celtics fans and the TD Garden environment during a recent livestream amid trade rumors.
Boston Celtics and Miami Heat are most speculated to be destinations for Antetokounmpo, who wishes to remain in the Eastern Conference.
Speculation involves whether Jaylen Brown would be moved in a potential trade for Antetokounmpo, possibly in a three-team deal.

title: Harvard ‘Senior Sunrise’ cut short by cops
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Police cleared Harvard's Senior Sunrise event on Weeks Footbridge due to crowd and safety concerns.
Massachusetts State Police responded at 5 a.m. after reports of attempts to swim in the Charles River.
Cambridge and Harvard University police assisted; no information on arrests or injuries was immediately available.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair' is streaming on Peacock, combining both volumes into a single film.
'The Truman Show' featuring Jim Carrey is available on Paramount+ and YouTube.
'The Four Seasons' Season 2, starring Tina Fey and Will Forte, is now streaming on Netflix.

title: Red Sox predicted to draft 18-year-old third baseman in 2026 MLB Draft by ESPN
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: ESPN's Kiley McDaniel predicts Red Sox will draft 18-year-old Bo Lowrance, a third baseman from South Carolina.
Lowrance, compared to MLB star Freddie Freeman, is a projected first-round pick at the MLB Draft on July 11.
Despite commitment to University of Virginia, Lowrance is noted for consistent hitting ability and potential position switch to first base.

title: Lowell father and daughter charged in alleged payroll tax scheme
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lim Ou, 69, and Nalen Ou, 39, from Lowell, were charged for allegedly hiding over $48 million in cash payroll.
The pair allegedly ran employment agencies and evaded over $12 million in federal employment taxes from 2019 to 2025.
If convicted, they face up to five years in prison, fines up to $250,000, and possible restitution.

title: Report: Christian Gonzalez unlikely to participate in OTAs under current Patriots contract
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Christian Gonzalez seeks a contract extension with the New England Patriots, aiming for top NFL cornerback pay.
Gonzalez missed Patriots voluntary OTAs, possibly holding out until an agreement on his contract is reached.
Patriots recently restructured Mike Onwenu's contract, freeing cap space, potentially aiding Gonzalez's contract negotiations.

title: Search underway for driver who led cops on a chase through Everett, Malden, Stoneham
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A suspect in a drug investigation led police on a chase through Everett, Malden, and Stoneham.
The fleeing vehicle struck a State Police cruiser and was abandoned in Stoneham, no injuries reported.
The driver with multiple outstanding warrants remains at large, prompting an active police search.

title: Ex-Patriots HC Jerod Mayo has a new job away from football
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jerod Mayo joins Fifth Down Capital as managing director, a private equity firm in Needham.
Mayo, former Patriots head coach, fired after a 4-13 season in 2024.
Before coaching, Mayo worked as Optum's VP of Business Development from 2015 to 2018.

title: Mass. man reports beating Mount Everest speed climbing record
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Tyler Andrews from Massachusetts broke the Mount Everest speed climbing record, reaching the summit in 9 hours and 55 minutes.
Andrews used supplemental oxygen during his climb, surpassing Lhakpa Gelu Sherpa's 2003 record by over an hour.
He dedicated the climb to his late friend Greg Krupa, a supporter of The Chaski Foundation, which Andrews co-founded.
